abstract: Launch, communicate with, and invalidate browser extensions.

# Extension lifecycle

**API Collection**

Launch, communicate with, and invalidate browser extensions.

## Essentials

- **Managing the browser extension life cycle**: Coordinate helper processes to efficiently support your browser app.
- **Using XPC to communicate with browser extensions**: Build interprocess communication between your host app and extensions.

## Browser extensions

- **WebContentExtension**: An interface for configuring a web content helper extension process that will carry web page decoding operations on behalf of the browser app.
- **WebContentExtensionConfiguration**
- **NetworkingExtension**: An interface for configuring a networking helper extension process that will carry out networking operations on behalf of the browser app.
- **NetworkingExtensionConfiguration**
- **RenderingExtension**: An interface for configuring a rendering helper extension process that will carry out operations requiring rendering access on behalf of the browser app.
- **RenderingExtensionConfiguration**

## Host app representations

- **WebContentProcess**: An object that represents a running web content extension process.
- **NetworkingProcess**: An object that represents a running browser networking extension process.
- **RenderingProcess**: An object that represents a running browser rendering extension process.

## Extension capabilities

- **ProcessCapability**: An enumeration that identifies capabilities that a browser app can grant to its extension processes.
- **MediaEnvironment**: An object that identifies a media playback or streaming environment.

## Browser extensions

- **Creating browser extensions in Xcode**: Configure your Xcode project to support your alternative browser engine.
- **Extension resources**: Control access to files and memory in browser extensions.
